comment_text.sn37.26:1.5: “Under her thumb”, literally, “having vanquished her husband” (sāmikaṁ pasayha), like Indra’s wife Sujā (Rig Veda 10.159; see note at SN 11.12:6.1).

root_text.sn37.26:1.4: imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca mātugāmassa balāni.
root_text.sn37.26:1.5: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi balehi samannāgato mātugāmo sāmikaṁ pasayha agāraṁ ajjhāvasatī”ti.

translation_text.sn37.26:1.1: “Mendicants, there are these five powers of a lady.
translation_text.sn37.26:1.3: Attractiveness, wealth, relatives, children, and ethical behavior.
translation_text.sn37.26:1.4: These are the five powers of a lady.
translation_text.sn37.26:1.5: A lady living at home with these five powers has her husband under her thumb.”
